evals.prompt: I need to create an Orion config to monitor control plane performance for cluster-density-v2 on AWS with OCP 4.18. I want to track API server CPU, etcd disk commit latency, and OVN kubernetes memory. We have 3 m6a.xlarge masters and 6 m6a.xlarge workers. My ES config is already set up at ~/.orion/elasticsearch-config.yaml.
evals.expected_output: A valid Orion YAML config with correct kube-burner structure: metricName fields, agg: with nested value/agg_type, node config metadata, correct namespaces for each component

assertions.name: uses-agg-not-aggregation
assertions.description: Config uses 'agg:' field with nested value/agg_type, not 'aggregation:'

assertions.name: includes-metricName
assertions.description: Each metric entry has a 'metricName' field

assertions.name: includes-node-config
assertions.description: Config includes masterNodesType, masterNodesCount, workerNodesType, workerNodesCount in metadata

assertions.name: correct-namespaces
assertions.description: Uses correct namespace filters (e.g., openshift-kube-apiserver for API server, openshift-ovn-kubernetes for OVN)

evals.prompt: Can you help me set up an Orion config for k8s-netperf analysis? I want to track TCP_STREAM and TCP_RR throughput and latency for pod network (not host network, no service) on AWS with OCP 4.17. I already have my ES connection configured.
evals.expected_output: A valid k8s-netperf Orion YAML config with: no aggregation field, profile/hostNetwork/service at metrics level (not metadata), quoted boolean strings, correct metadata structure with metadata.platform and metadata.ocpMajorVersion

assertions.name: no-aggregation-field
assertions.description: Config does NOT contain an 'aggregation' or 'agg' field (k8s-netperf data is pre-aggregated)

assertions.name: filters-at-metrics-level
assertions.description: profile.keyword, hostNetwork, and service fields are at the metrics level, not inside metadata

assertions.name: quoted-booleans
assertions.description: Boolean values for hostNetwork and service use quoted strings like '"false"', not bare YAML booleans

assertions.name: correct-metadata-prefix
assertions.description: Metadata fields use metadata.platform and metadata.ocpMajorVersion prefix format

assertions.name: covers-both-profiles
assertions.description: Config includes separate metric entries for both TCP_STREAM and TCP_RR profiles

evals.prompt: I'm getting no data returned when I run orion --config my-config.yaml --hunter-analyze against my ES cluster. The config has benchmark.keyword set to 'cluster-density' and I'm using the ripsaw-kube-burner-* index. My ES config validates fine. What could be wrong and how do I debug this?
evals.expected_output: Should identify that 'cluster-density' is likely wrong (should be 'cluster-density-v2'), suggest using the discovery script to find correct benchmark names, recommend debugging steps including --debug flag and checking metadata filters

assertions.name: identifies-wrong-benchmark-name
assertions.description: Identifies that 'cluster-density' is likely incorrect and should be 'cluster-density-v2'

assertions.name: suggests-discovery-script
assertions.description: Recommends using the discovery script (discover-es-data.py) to find correct benchmark names

assertions.name: suggests-debug-flag
assertions.description: Mentions using the --debug flag for detailed query/processing information

assertions.name: mentions-metadata-filters
assertions.description: Discusses verifying metadata filter values match what exists in ES
